macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Indian Markets Plunge as GIFT Nifty Drops 800 Points Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Indian stock markets experienced a sharp decline on March 9, 2026, with GIFT Nifty trading 800 points lower, driven by global sell-offs, rising geopolitical tensions, and surging Brent crude oil prices at $118 per barrel. Sensex and Nifty are set to open significantly lower following a 1%+ drop on March 6.

items.title: US Stocks Decline Sharply with Surging Oil and VIX Volatility
items.summary: US stocks are down amid spiking oil prices above $100, VIX surging past 30 signaling high uncertainty and expected 2% daily S&P 500 moves, raising stagflation risks. Investors eye opportunities in Alphabet and Palantir as tech rotates.
items.source: YouTube (Stocks Are Down — Here’s How to Profit Now)

items.title: Iran War Impacts US Markets, Inflation, and Fed Policy
items.summary: The war in Iran is pressuring US stock markets, boosting oil and defense stocks while heightening inflation risks that could influence the Federal Reserve's interest-rate decisions. Morningstar discusses positioning portfolios amid market rotation.

items.title: Nifty and Bank Nifty Predictions Amid Sensex Volatility
items.summary: Market analysis for March 10, 2026, highlights Sensex levels at 77,000-78,200 with potential drops to 76,000-76,500 on red candles; Tata Steel noted for positive news and upward momentum.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $70K Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly pushed higher but slipped back below $70K due to war tensions, oil shocks, and weak jobs data, raising concerns about its next direction. Markets are turning volatile again.[1]

items.title: CLARITY Act Nears Final Countdown
items.summary: The CLARITY Act is in its final stages amid ongoing disputes between banks and crypto firms over stablecoin yields. Discussions also highlight AI-driven layoffs in tech and finance.[1]

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Sideways at Around $67,500
items.summary: Bitcoin remains in a range since February 5th, sitting at $67,500, with sideways movement amid geopolitical tensions and on-chain data showing supply in loss for BTC, ETH, and XRP.[4]

items.title: XRP at $134 in Sideways Range Post-Capitulation
items.summary: XRP holds at $134 within its established range, following high selling and buying volume, with on-chain losses peaking at 38 billion coins.[4]

items.title: GENIUS Act Pushes for Crypto Clarity Legislation
items.summary: Efforts are underway for the GENIUS Act to provide regulatory clarity, alongside Kraken tokenizing NASDAQ stocks and new ETFs like KRP tracking CoinDesk 20 index.[5]

items.title: Crypto Rebound Discussed Amid Geopolitical Turmoil
items.summary: Investors assess cryptocurrency rebound potential with new ETFs amid geopolitical tests, distinguishing Bitcoin as store of value versus Ethereum's blockchain utility.[5]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Microsoft Launches Nine New AI Certifications for 2026 Including AI-901 Replacing AI-900
items.summary: Microsoft has introduced nine new certifications focusing on Generative AI, Agentic AI, MLOps, and GenAIOps, with AI-900 being replaced by AI-901 and others like AI-300 for AI operations. These updates target Azure AI engineers, architects, and data professionals preparing for cloud AI roles.

items.title: USC Researchers Develop AI That Fixes Its Own Knowledge Gaps in Real Time
items.summary: USC Viterbi researchers created a method allowing AI models to dramatically improve performance on untrained data by recompiling and retrying, achieving up to 96% accuracy beyond initial training limits. The approach unlocks latent capabilities through structured feedback.

items.title: MIT Study Discovers Brain Neurons Use Precision Error Signals Similar to AI Backpropagation
items.summary: MIT researchers found mice neurons receive vectorized instructive signals during learning akin to machine learning's backpropagation, using brain-computer interfaces to study parallels between biological and artificial neural networks. This bridges neuroscience and AI for better brain-inspired models.

items.title: Companies Shift to Testing Employee AI Fluency as Next Phase of AI Adoption
items.summary: Major corporations, using platforms like Workera serving 10% of Fortune 500, are now assessing employee skills in AI, generative AI, and responsible AI beyond basic adoption, including prompting, bias recognition, and understanding AI agents.

items.title: New Method Improves AI Models' Ability to Explain Predictions Using Learned Concepts
items.summary: Researchers developed a technique that extracts and forces AI models to use pre-learned concepts for better explainability of predictions, enhancing transparency without retraining.

items.title: Study Shows Mice and AI Agents Learn Cooperation Through Shared Principles
items.summary: Scientists observed mice and AI independently developing similar coordination strategies in cooperative tasks, highlighting common learning mechanisms.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Legends of Runeterra Patch 7.3 'With Friends Like These' Announced
items.summary: Riot Games released a dev snapshot for Patch 7.3, introducing a new Delore PVE-exclusive champion, Path of Champions rewards, collaboration with Riftbound teams, and upcoming quality-of-life improvements like 2x animation speed.

items.title: Indie Gaming Highlights: New Releases for March 9-15, 2026
items.summary: Weekly indie roundup features upcoming Steam releases including Yuma Nest, Connected Clue, Crabmeat, Desktop Raid, Funi Raccoon Game, Galactic Vault, ITER-8, Rhell: Warped Worlds & Troubled Times, Sherman Commander, The Liar Princess and the Blind Prince, and Two Strikes - Musashi Miyamoto. Surgent Studios releasing Fix Force this week.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Qualcomm Partners with Neura Robotics for Next-Gen Robots
items.summary: Qualcomm has partnered with German robotics startup Neura Robotics to develop advanced robots using Qualcomm’s Dragonwing Robotics IQ10 processors for autonomous mobile robots and humanoids. Neura will integrate its Neuraverse platform to test and fine-tune these systems.

items.title: Faraday Future to Present at Cantor Global Tech Conference
items.summary: Faraday Future's Global President Jerry Wang will participate in Cantor's 2nd Annual Global Technology & Industrial Growth Conference on March 10-11, 2026, discussing strategic roadmap, FX Super One vehicle, and embodied AI robotics products.
